title: Restoring deleted files in Files column
excerpt: So far, Monday does not have the ability to restore deleted files from files column. While browsing the file, it happens many time that you click the icon x and the file is suddenly gone. Since there is on confirmation s…

posts.author: Altuijri (Ahmad)
posts.cookedHtml:
So far, Monday does not have the ability to restore deleted files from files column. While browsing the file, it happens many time that you click the icon x and the file is suddenly gone. Since there is on confirmation step, your file could be lost easily.
I would love to see Monday has the ability to restore deleted files

posts.author: lost_gweedo (Will Strand)
posts.cookedHtml: I would really love this ability-to restore deleted files. Or, as an alternative, flashing the “undo” button with the countdown to delete like we have elsewhere within Monday. Thanks.

posts.author: DenstadConsulting (Thomas Denstad)
posts.cookedHtml:
Jepp,
just deleted a file from the Files column by mistake.
It was stupid of me to accidentally delete that important file, but even more stupid that I cant get that file back from the recycle bin.
Is there any new updates on this? Can the file be found somewhere now?

posts.author: mariakorr (Maria)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi! @Altuijri @DenstadConsulting @lost_gweedo
Here in Rewind, my team and I are building a backup and restore solution for Monday.
With it, you’ll be able to backup and recover all your data as well as files.

Please reach out to me. I’d love to have a chat with you to hear more about your backup needs! Here’s my email: maria.korolenko [at]rewind. io
Here’s more information about backups for Monday: https: //rewind. com/products/backups/monday/

posts.author: Krystal (Krystal O'halloran)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Maria, any progress with this? I’m fairly new to using Monday and I just accidently deleted a whole lot of important information from several columns. My mistake however it’s very frustrating that I’m unable to retrieve all of the missing information and that it’s that easy for it to be deleted and gone forever.

posts.author: DannyG23 (Danny Glennon)
posts.cookedHtml:
@mariakorr - Any update on retrieving deleted files from a column? Or at least having a deletion verification to avoid accidental losses of files stored in an item’s file column? Any help of update here is greatly appreciated!

Thanks!

posts.author: acrosby (Alex Crosby)
posts.cookedHtml: This is an essential feature!! You should be able to ‘undo’ actions from the items activity log.

posts.author: drescheg (Guy Drescher)
posts.cookedHtml: Not being able to recover files seems to make the feature of attaching files to items risky at best. How can you have a legit work os if it does not include the basics of a file server?

posts.author: Kouji (Maverick Ho)
posts.cookedHtml: The file attached within the column can be accidentally delete and there is no way to restore it. We won’t be able get to get back the information that being accidentally or intentionally deleted!!!

posts.author: Will_from_Pro_Backup (Will Dewulf)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Kouji , our team has built Pro Backup for monday.com that can restore files linked to a column field.

Here’s a tutorial video that explains you how to do this: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DAopcKXU7J4

posts.author: gp42 (Gennady Potapov)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, Sync for Google Drive app synchronises files to Goggle Drive. It can work as a backup mechanism, since all deleted files end up in Google Drive waste bin, and can be easily recovered within 30 days.

posts.author: sara.johnston (Sara Johnston)
posts.cookedHtml: It is absolutely critical that we have the ability to restore deleted files! My team has been using the Files Column more and more because we love the version history functionality and the visibility. Files we attach here are usually more important than files we attach in updates or directly in the Files library. It is totally unacceptable for these files to not follow the same deletion procedure as all the others.

posts.author: spharr (Sarah Pharr)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be reasonable and highly beneficial if Files were included in the list of things that can be restored from Trash. Especially considering how easy it is to delete them by mistake.

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey users!

You could try our Google Drive Integration+Sync app.

You can set automations to automatically sync files, each time a new one is added.

Could help keep a backup.
